topic: Someone receives a friend request from a childhood bully
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a jarring splash of blue on my screen. A profile picture loaded: a familiar, sneering grin I hadn't seen in fifteen years. My jaw tightened almost involuntarily. *Seriously?* I stared at the request, my thumb hovering over the 'decline' button, a muscle ticking in my cheek. It was Mark. The architect of my awkward middle school years. A slow, simmering irritation began to bubble in my chest. Why now? What did he want? I let the message sit there, a coiled spring ready to snap, before finally tapping decline with unnecessary force.

[story 2]

I glanced at my phone, the vibration a tiny, insistent buzz against the desk. Another incoming connection. My knuckles were white where I gripped the edge of the wooden surface. This had to be him. The sheer audacity of it made my stomach clench. I leaned back in my chair, forcing a deep, slow breath, trying to steady the rapid flutter beneath my ribs. *Just ignore it. Don't give him the satisfaction.* But the urge to see what nonsense he’d concocted was overwhelming. My eyes darted back to the screen, the silence in the room suddenly thick and suffocating.

[story 3]

The little chime sounded, pulling my attention away from the spreadsheet I was trying to finalize. A familiar name flashed across the display, accompanied by a request to connect. I sighed, a sharp, audible release of air. My hands started to tap a quick, restless rhythm on the keyboard. This was taking up valuable time. I felt a distinct tightening in my shoulders, a need to dismiss this intrusion immediately. I didn't even bother reading the profile; I just swiped to reject the invitation, eager for the digital noise to stop.
